subject: Re: WarpTools ts_export_particles failes with half of the tilt series
body:
Hi Alister,

Thanks a lot. I’ll try to work it out. I’ll let you and the rest of the
group know if I find a solution.

Best,
Arsen

On Mon, Dec 2, 2024 at 3:10 AM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Arsen,
>
> Sorry but you’re working outside of the standard workflow enough that
> you’ll have to figure things out yourself - it sounds like either a problem
> with your conversion or a misuse of the options in ts_export_particles
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Dec 1, 2024, at 13:56, Arsen Petrovic <arsenpetrovic74@googlemail.com>
> wrote:
>
> ﻿
> Hi Alister,
>
> Thanks for your prompt reply!
>
> Essentially, I have imported in Warp  previously processed tilt series. I
> have already obtained relatively “decent “ resolution (~10Å) from the same
> data set in STOPGAP, and wanted to transfer to the WARP/Relion/M pipeline
> in order to push things a bit further, if possible.
>
> I have converted the STOPGAP .star motivelist to the Relion .star format.
> When I try to extract the subvolumes in 3D (WarpTools ts_export_particles),
> for some reason, subvolumes are not extracted from several tilt series
> (i.e. in 2/18 they are marked as unselected). I tried to use the WarpTools
> command change_selection to mark these tomograms as selected, but as soon
> as I start the ts_export_particles command, the same tomograms are
> automatically marked as unselected.
>
> Occasionally, when I restart the ts_export_particles some additional
> tomograms that were previously OK become unselected.
>
> Thanks a lot for your help.
>
> Best,
> Arsen
>
> On Sun, Dec 1, 2024 at 5:29 PM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Arsen,
>>
>> I never heard back from Juha so could not investigate further. Could you
>> please provide some more information like the commands you used, whether
>> those TS are different in any way? (Lots of contamination? Particularly bad
>> tracking?)
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>
>> On Dec 1, 2024, at 08:14, 'Arsen Petrovic' via Warp <
>> warp-em@googlegroups.com> wrote:
>>
>> ﻿
>> Hi all,
>>
>> I have the same problem. Did someone find the solution?
>>
>> Thanks a lot!
>>
>> Best,
>> Arsen
>>
>>
>>
>> On Friday, October 18, 2024 at 9:02:35 AM UTC+2 juha.hu...@gmail.com
>> wrote:
>>
>> Hello,
>>
>> I am running WarpTools ts_export_particles.  For some reason, 15/31 tilt
>> series fail.  The tomogram reconstruction and template matching steps work
>> fine for all 31.  Any suggestions on what to check?
>>
>> The command and output are below.
>>
>> WarpTools ts_export_particles \
>> --settings warp_tiltseries.settings \
>> --input_directory warp_tiltseries/matching \
>> --input_pattern "*run_class001_clean.star" \
>> --normalized_coords \
>> --output_processing relion_new2 \
>> --output_star relion_new2/matching.star \
>> --output_angpix 9.62 \
>> --box 64 \
>> --diameter 300 \
>> --relative_output_paths \
>> --2d
>>
>> Running command ts_export_particles with:
>> input_star = null
>> input_directory = warp_tiltseries/matching
>> input_pattern = *run_class001_clean.star
>> coords_angpix = null
>> normalized_coords = True
>> output_star = relion_new2/matching.star
>> output_angpix = 9.62
>> box = 64
>> diameter = 300
>> relative_output_paths = True
>> 2d = True
>> 3d = False
>> dont_normalize_input = False
>> dont_normalize_3d = False
>> n_tilts = null
>> max_missing_tilts = 5
>> device_list = {  }
>> perdevice = 1
>> workers = {  }
>> settings = warp_tiltseries.settings
>> input_data = {  }
>> input_data_recursive = False
>> input_processing = null
>> output_processing = relion_new2
>> input_norawdata = False
>>

>> warp_tiltseries.settings

>> 0/31
